channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanga Region Councils Work to Improve Revenue Collection with Korogwe Leading
item.description: Local government authorities in Tanga Region are struggling to meet revenue targets while still funding key development projects. Between July 2025 and February 2026, councils collected 32.45 billion ...

item.title: Vancouver Police Investigate Possible Evidence Tampering in High-Profile Murder Case
item.description: Vancouver police are conducting an investigation, known as Project Loyalty, into whether someone with access to the department's property office colluded with organized criminals to tamper with eviden...

item.title: Imran Khan’s sons may visit Pakistan using NICOP after Jemima Goldsmith’s appeal
item.description: On 18th March 2026, Pakistan’s Information Minister, Attaullah Tarar, confirmed that the sons of former Prime Minister Imran Khan, namely Kasim and Suleiman, are allowed to visit Pakistan using their ...

item.title: Kid Rock questions DOJ settlement in Ticketmaster antitrust case
item.description: Kid Rock, a longtime supporter of Donald Trump, has publicly criticized the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) following its settlement in a major antitrust case involving Ticketmaster and its parent co...

item.title: Chalmers outlines May 12 budget focused on tax reform, productivity, and savings
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has pledged that the upcoming federal budget, to be delivered on May 12, will prioritise fiscal responsibility, tax reform, and boosting productivity. Speaking to the Australian...

item.title: Pakistan dismisses India’s statement over counter-terrorism strikes in Afghanistan
item.description: On Wednesday, Pakistan’s Foreign Office officially rejected India’s statement condemning Pakistan’s airstrikes against terrorist infrastructure in Afghanistan. The strikes, carried out overnight in Ka...

item.title: South East Development Commission Pledges Financial Support for 2026 National Sports Festival in Enugu
item.description: The 2026 National Sports Festival, wey dem tag Coal City Games, for Enugu State don dey receive plenty support as South East Development Commission (SEDC) come join as the latest partner. Mark Okoye, ...

item.title: Strengthening Collaboration Between Government and NGOs Key to Reducing Gender-Based Violence in South Africa
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa declared gender-based violence and femicide a national disaster in November 2025, signalling urgency in tackling a long-standing crisis in South Africa. Despite this, the iss...

item.title: West Asia Conflict Updates: Ships Navigate Strait of Hormuz Amid Blockade, Projectile Hits Near Bushehr Nuclear Plant with No Damage
item.description: The ongoing conflict in West Asia, involving Israel, the United States, and Iran, continues to escalate with significant developments reported on March 18, 2026. Israel's Defence Minister claimed that...

item.title: Shehu Sani Criticizes CAF for Reversing Senegal’s AFCON 2025 Win
item.description: Nigeria former senator, Shehu Sani don blast CAF dem proper over wetin happen for AFCON 2025 final. CAF come decide say Senegal no go carry the trophy again because dem players waka comot from pitch s...

item.title: Delhi Fire in Palam Claims 9 Lives; Authorities Question Fire Safety in Old Family Home
item.description: A tragic fire in Palam, Delhi has claimed the lives of nine members of a single family spanning three generations. The incident has raised serious concerns about fire safety in densely populated homes...

item.title: Graaff-Reinet residents divided over renaming town after Robert Sobukwe
item.description: The historic Karoo town of Graaff-Reinet is in the midst of a heated debate following a government decision to rename it after Robert Sobukwe, a celebrated anti-apartheid activist and founding member ...

item.title: Meru Residents Collect Milk from Overturned Tanker After Accident
item.description: On Wednesday, March 18, 2026, chaotic scenes unfolded along Kanyuru Road in Meru Town when a tanker carrying milk developed brake failure and rolled into a nearby ditch. Locals quickly rushed to colle...

item.title: Kenyan Police Complete Second Deployment in Haiti, Return Home Safely
item.description: Nairobi — Kenya has officially welcomed back the second batch of its police officers who were deployed to Haiti under the Multinational Security Support Mission. A total of 215 officers returned safel...

item.title: Pakistan prolongs airspace closure for Indian aircraft until April 24
item.description: Pakistan’s civil aviation authorities have decided to extend the closure of the country’s airspace to all Indian-registered aircraft until April 24, 2026, according to a fresh Notam issued by the Paki...

item.title: First Dog on the Moon cartoon satirises invasive fire ants impacting Queensland
item.description: This piece is a satirical cartoon by First Dog on the Moon, published in The Guardian's Comment is Free section on 18 March 2026. The cartoon humorously exaggerates the role of invasive ants—likely re...

item.title: Ishaq Dar to attend Riyadh meeting, calls for dialogue to resolve Middle East crisis
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister of Pakistan, Ishaq Dar, is scheduled to leave for Riyadh on 18th March for a meeting with foreign ministers from the region. The focus of this visit is to ur...

item.title: Ex-Cellmate Letter Alleges Links Between Matlala and High-Ranking Officials

item.title: Eight members of a family die in Indore house fire caused by electric vehicle explosion
item.description: Early on Wednesday, 18th March 2026, a tragic incident occurred in Indore where eight members of a family, including two children, lost their lives in a fire that broke out due to an explosion at an e...
